visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you wish to visit Sweden for cultural events you need to apply for Schengen visa.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If you wish to visit Sweden for cultural events you need to apply for Schengen visa.

## Visa Fees
Visa fee will only be accepted in Jordanian Dinar (JOD) and the application center will follow the exchange rate provided by the embassy.

[Table:    Cultural ]

## SERVICE FEES
EUR 20 per application
The above fees are non-refundable
                                
Exemption of Visa Fee
- Children under Six years of age.
- School pupils, students, research students and accompanying teachers on study or training trips.
- Third-country researchers travelling for the purpose of pursuing scientific research.
- Holders of PA VIP passports on the condition that a) the visa application is supported by a note verbale from the protocol department of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Palestinian authority and b) if the holder of the
                                        VIP passport is a diplomat or a high ranking official.
- Representatives of non-governmental organisations who are aged 25 or under, who take part in seminars, conferences, sports events, cultural events or educational events organised by NGOs.
- Persons with an invitation from SIDA or who is going to attend a training etc. through another organisation that is funded by SIDA (unless the invitation state that the applicant should pay the fee).
- Persons with an invitation from the Swedish Institute.
- UN Travel Document holders – only regarding business trips.
- Family member of citizens of the EU, Norway, Iceland, Liechtenstein or Switzerland, if they travel together with them or will be meeting them in connection with the trip. Family member of Swedish citizens have to pay a fee for visa
                                        applications.
- Citizens of countries that have signed visa facilitation agreements with the EU pay a reduced fee or are exempt from payment. This applies to citizens of:

[Table:    Cultural ]

## Documents Required
To help us process your application quickly and accurately, please complete the Sweden Visa Application Form (VAF) electronically using the editable PDF available on this site, print the typed copy and carry it with appointment letter during your visit to the visa application centre. Completing the form electronically reduces counter waiting time and helps avoid errors caused by illegible handwriting.
Note: Applicants who arrive without a form completed electronically will still be accepted with a completed handwritten form; however, they should expect additional processing time at Visa Application Centre.
SWEDEN VISA APPLICATION FORM - ENGLISH
SWEDEN VISA APPLICATION FORM - SWEDISH
Essentials:
Please click on each of the above tab for detailed information.
- Every person should hold a personal travelling document and must complete a separate application form.
- Minor children must submit an application form signed by a person with parental authority or by a legal guardian;
Please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3uPnrDP0Wah9Lqf4CAdPN6/b30dc4e324a9d787c67074a1e13d0e47/Sweden-visa_checklist_2021.pdf) to download checklist 
Attention :
Your application will not be admissible and will thus not be examined if you do not lodge a completed, signed application form, a valid travel document and a photograph, if you do not pay the visa fees and if you do not agree to  give your biometric data.
                                

## Photo Specifications
Applicants must submit Application form with the new photo format, as explained below: 
General
- The photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization).
- Photo must have plain white background.
- The photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Not tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly.
- Close up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80% of the photograph.
- Photo in sharp focus and clear.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure: 
- The picture dimensions must be 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A smaller picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 235 pixels by 320 pixels is not valid.
- On the other hand, the photo should keep a proportion of 3 x 4 (width x height) within limits. However, 250 x 340 pixels picture would be valid, but not a 290 x 340 pixels one.
- Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.
Resolution: 
- An image resolution is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used to control printing quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better printing quality.
- The resolution of the photos should be 300 dpi, so, when you scan the image, check this parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution, the bigger the picture.
- The only image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Grup Format). The other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected (a warning will be shown).
Attaching the Photo to the Application form 
Glue one photograph to the application form in the designated space.
Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph presented does not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
All visa applications are handled and decided on by the Regional Migration Hub at the Swedish Embassy in Amman, Jordan. The Embassy urges all applicants to apply well in advance of the intended entry into the Schengen area. The visa processing time is 15 working days from the date when the applications reach the Regional Migration Hub. Note that it also takes time to transfer the applications to and from the Regional Migration Hub in Amman, in total at least 25-30 days from submission of application at VFS until the passport may be picked up.
                              

## Download Forms
Visa Application Form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/7l3Z43SaEWMBZ7h5sWZVan/6a1d9591f6615071d7c0ff7c3b1bc571/application-for-schengen-visa.pdf) (English Version)
Consent (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3LVyHpdkmBLkRDOzPIdAfo/42265a043bcead4ef58a5afdc1b43d93/Consent_230614.pdf) for minors traveling without parents/guardians.

Your application will not be admissible and will thus not be examined if you do not lodge a completed, signed application form, a valid travel document and a photograph, if you do not pay the visa fees and if you do not agree to

## Photo Specifications
Applicants must submit Application form with the new photo format, as explained below: 
General
- The photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization).
- Photo must have plain white background.
- The photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Not tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly.
- Close up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80% of the photograph.
- Photo in sharp focus and clear.

The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure: 
- The picture dimensions must be 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A smaller picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 235 pixels by 320 pixels is not valid.

The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure: 
- The picture dimensions must be 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A smaller picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 235 pixels by 320 pixels is not valid.
- On the other hand, the photo should keep a proportion of 3 x 4 (width x height) within limits. However, 250 x 340 pixels picture would be valid, but not a 290 x 340 pixels one.
- Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.
Resolution: 
- An image resolution is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used to control printing quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better printing quality.
- The resolution of the photos should be 300 dpi, so, when you scan the image, check this parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution, the bigger the picture.
- The only image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Grup Format). The other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected (a warning will be shown).
Attaching the Photo to the Application form 
Glue one photograph to the application form in the designated space.
Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph presented does not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
All visa applications are handled and decided on by the Regional Migration Hub at the Swedish Embassy in Amman, Jordan. The Embassy urges all applicants to apply well in advance of the intended entry into the Schengen area. The visa processing time is 15 working days from the date when the applications reach the Regional Migration Hub. Note that it also takes time to transfer the applications to and from the Regional Migration Hub in Amman, in total at least 25-30 days from submission of application at VFS until the passport may be picked up.

## Overview
If you wish to visit Sweden to receive medical treatment and are a citizen of a country outside the EU, you will in most cases need a visa.

## Visa Fees
Visa fee will only be accepted in Jordanian Dinar (JOD) and the application center will follow the exchange rate provided by the embassy.

[Table:    Medical reasons ]

## SERVICE FEES
EUR 20 per application
The above fees are non-refundable 
Exemption of Visa Fee
- Children under Six years of age.
- School pupils, students, research students and accompanying teachers on study or training trips.
- Third-country researchers travelling for the purpose of pursuing scientific research.

                                        VIP passport is a diplomat or a high ranking official.
- Representatives of non-governmental organisations who are aged 25 or under, who take part in seminars, conferences, sports events, cultural events or educational events organised by NGOs.
- Persons with an invitation from SIDA or who is going to attend a training etc. through another organisation that is funded by SIDA (unless the invitation state that the applicant should pay the fee).
- Persons with an invitation from the Swedish Institute.
- UN Travel Document holders – only regarding business trips.
- Family member of citizens of the EU, Norway, Iceland, Liechtenstein or Switzerland, if they travel together with them or will be meeting them in connection with the trip. Family member of Swedish citizens have to pay a fee for visa
                                        applications.
- Citizens of countries that have signed visa facilitation agreements with the EU pay a reduced fee or are exempt from payment. This applies to citizens of:
